sdo:itemDesc.dc:title: Handwritten letter from Oppel to Schenker, dated April 27, 1933
sdo:itemDesc.dc:description: Oppel discusses the consecutive fifths in Beethoven’s Quartet Op. 74. -- He has been reading books on music by Güldenstein (which discusses Schenker) and Achtélik, neither of which he can recommend. -- A pupil of his has compiled a list of Schenkerian terms and hopes to present them in a radio broadcast.

tei:opener.tei:salute: Mein lieber Herr Professor Schenker,Receipt of this letter is recorded in Schenker’s diary for April 29, 1933: “Von Oppel (Br.): Dank für Brahms; über ein Buch von Güldenstein, in dem sich der Autor auch mit mir auseinandersetzt; über die vortreffliche Analyse eines Schülers, in meinem Sinne verfaßt” (“From Oppel (letter): thanks for Brahms; concerning a book by Güldenstein, in which the author takes issue with me; concerning an excellent analysis by one of his pupils, made according to my principles”).

tei:div.tei:p: herzlichen Dank für die Brahms 5 u 8 Studie!Oppel had been engaged since August 1929 (see OJ 13/10, [82] and subsequent correspondence) in a close examination for Schenker of Brahms’s study of parallel 5ths and octaves in music of the 16th to 19th centuries. Oppel’s work is nowhere acknowledged in the 1933 publication. Ich habe mich sehr gefreut, dass diese Arbeit nun allgemein zugänglich ist. Bei Beeth op 74 gibt Altmann in d. kl. Eulenburgpartitur tatsächlich f im Cello an, eine schlechte Correctur, denn der Sinn ist u. bleibt doch music example, während music example f 2mal dem g das gewollte Übergewicht schmälern würde.The two music examples reproduce the cello part of Beethoven’s Quartet in E, Op. 74, first movement, bars 94–96 of the cello part. In the first example, Oppel has simplified the part to show the preponderance of a bass G (as the dominant of C); in the second, he reproduces the passage in Altmann’s edition, in which the first note of bar 95 is given as F, thus removing the parallel fifths between the first violin and cello. (The first edition of Op. 74 and the old and new Beethoven collected editions all give G.) –

Ich hätte längst wieder geschrieben, aber auch Ostern sass ich in Arbeit. Die Partei verschlingt viel Zeit, u. ich musste, um klar zu sehn u. zu handeln, die Vorgeschichte unsrer Anstalt studieren seit der Gründung durch Mendelssohn 1843.By “Partei”, Oppel almost certainly means the National Socialists, although he does not say so explicitly, nor does his remark confirm that he was actually a member of the Nazi Party at the time. In a letter written three weeks later, OJ 13/10, [167], May 21, 1933, he tells Schenker he is working in a “cell” that is tasked with writing – or rewriting? – the history of the Leipzig Conservatory, which had been founded 90 years before by Felix Mendelssohn, who was Jewish by birth. Cf. OJ 13/10, [172], July 6, 1933, in which he expresses approval of Hitler. Verwaltungstechnische u. rechtliche Fragen u. Schwierigkeiten harren noch der Lösung u. alles geht bei dem Riesenandrang zur Partei langsam, sehr langsam. – Von Gustav Güldenstein erschien ein Buch „Theorie der Tonart“ im Verlag E. Klett, Stuttgart, in dem er sich im Kapitel VII (die Chromatik) S. 159–174 auch mit Ihnen auseinandersetzt;Gustav Güldenstein, Theorie der Tonart (Stuttgart: Klett, 1920); a second edition appeared in 1928. Güldenstein (1888–1972) taught music theory at the Basel Conservatory (now the City of Basel Music Academy) from 1921 to 1953. ich bitte Sie, hauen Sie dem Waisenknaben gelegentlich eine kräftige Ohrfeige herunter. – Mein Schüler Goos hat mir neulich eine grosse Freude bereitet: er hat eine vorzügliche Analyse vom Präludium
der Suite C-dur für Cello solo v. Bach geliefert; ausserdem hatte er aus Ihren Werken „Schenkerworte“ zusammengestellt, um sie beim Rundfunk in der „nationalen“ Stunde anzubringen. Hoffentlich gelingt es ihm. –
Meiner Frau u. den Kindern geht’s gut. – Wie ich aus den Anmerkungen ersah, ist der „fr S“ drückfertig. Und ich schreie dazu dreimal kräftig: Hurrah! Dann kommt er im Herbst wohl noch heraus? –

Leider las ich gestern u. heute von Josef Achtélik „der Naturklang als Wurzel aller Harmonien“ (Kahnt 1922 – Leipzig).Josef Achtélig (1881–1965), German composer and choral conductor, was appointed to this post in 1933, the year in which he joined the Nazi Party. Übel, gänzlich verfehlt einseitig harmonisch. Verlorene Zeit. Aber da der Mann hier Vorsitzender des Reichsverbandes deutscher Tonkünstler ist, hielt ich für meine Pflicht, mir sein krummes Gewächs einmal anzusehn. –

Wie steht es mit Ihren Sommerplänen? Wollen Sie wieder nach Galtür? Ich hoffe, dass ich diesmal wieder in die Berge komme, obwohl die Geldfrage noch sehr in schwarzen Wolken hängt; aber nach diesem scharfen Winter brauche ich eine gründliche Erholung.

tei:closer.tei:salute: Alles Gute u. die herzlichsten Grüsse

tei:div.tei:p: Cordial thanks for Brahms’s study of consecutive fifths and octaves! I am very glad that this work is now generally available. In Beethoven’s Op. 74, Altmann actually gives F in the cello part: a poor correction, since the sense of the passage is always music example whereas in music example the twofold repetition of F would diminish the intended predominance of G.

I would have written again long ago, but I was stuck in work even over Easter. The Party consumes much of my time; and, in order to see and deal with things clearly, I had to study the prehistory of our institution since its founding by Mendelssohn in 1843. Administrative-technical and judicial questions and difficulties await a solution, and everything goes slowly, very slowly, under the gigantic pressure towards the Party. – A book by Gustav Güldenstein, Theory of Key, has been published by Klett in Stuttgart, in which the author even discusses your work, in Chapter 7 (“Chromaticism”), pp. 159–74; please give this orphan boy a good cuff around the ears sometime. – My pupil Goos has recently given me great pleasure: he has supplied an excellent analysis of the Prelude
from the C major Suite for solo cello by Bach; in addition, he has put together a collection of “Schenker words” from your writings, so that he can introduce them on the radio in the “national” hour. I hope that he will succeed.
My wife and children are well – As I saw from the notices, Free Composition is ready to go to press. And for this I shout three times: Hurrah! So, it will probably be published in the autumn?

Unfortunately, I read from Josef Achtélik’s The Chord of Nature as the Root of All Harmonies (Leipzig: Kahnt, 1922) yesterday. Bad: totally, mistakably and one-sidedly harmonic. A waste of time. But as the man is the local chairman of the Imperial Association of German Musicians, I regarded it as my duty to have a look at his fraudulent production. –

How are your summer plans shaping up? Are you hoping to go to Galtür again? I hope that I can get to the mountains again this time, although the question of money still hangs very much in dark clouds. After this harsh winter, however, I need a thorough recovery.

tei:closer.tei:salute: All good wishes, and the most cordial greetings
to the two of you from all of us!
